message: I've been digging into some solid fire safety basics lately, and I found myself actually learning things I didn't expect—like how much a little planning really does make a difference. There's a great government resource out there that walks through the practical stuff: escape planning, smoke alarms, what to do if there's a fire in your home. Nothing scary, just clear, useful information. Have you and your family ever actually talked through an escape plan together, or is that something you've been meaning to get around to?
